From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian steeplechase runner

Jekaterina Patjuk

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Nationality | [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") |
| Born | (1983-04-06) 6 April 1983 (age 42)  [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") |
| Sport | |
| Country | [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") |
| Sport | [Athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") |
| Event | [Steeplechase runner](/wiki/Steeplechase_(athletics) "Steeplechase (athletics)") |
| Achievements and titles | |
| Personal best | * 3000 m steeplechase: 9:50.15 (2014) |

**Jekaterina Patjuk** (born 6 April 1983) is an [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") athlete who competed mainly in the [3000 metres steeplechase](/wiki/Steeplechase_(athletics) "Steeplechase (athletics)").

She is the current [Estonian record](/wiki/Estonian_records_in_athletics "Estonian records in athletics") holder on [3000 metres steeplechase](/wiki/Steeplechase_(athletics) "Steeplechase (athletics)").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jekaterina_Patjuk&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

She won at least eight times national championships outdoor (two on [1500 metres](/wiki/1500_metres "1500 metres") and six on [5000 metres](/wiki/5000_metres "5000 metres")),[[1]](#cite_note-1) and at least (till 2006) five times indoor (two in 1500 m. and three on [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres"))[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 2020, she competed in the women's race at the [2020 World Athletics Half Marathon Championships](/wiki/2020_World_Athletics_Half_Marathon_Championships "2020 World Athletics Half Marathon Championships") held in Gdynia, Poland.[[3]](#cite_note-women_half_marathon_championships_2020-3)
